title: rubus × orthacanthophorus
intro: Rubus × orthacanthophorus, also known as the black raspberry, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the rose family and is a cross between the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis) and the thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus). The medicinal properties of black raspberry are attributed to its high content of anthocyanins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of black raspberry include reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Black raspberry can be prepared as a tea, juice, or supplement, and is also used in herbal preparations such as salves and tinctures to promote wound healing and skin health.
